items.title: Trump Deals Set To Fall Short of Sweeping Trade Reforms | Bloomberg: The Asia Trade 6/30/25
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>"Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets.

Chapters:
00:00:00 - Bloomberg: The Asia Trade begins
00:02:55 - Trump deals poised to fall short of sweeping global trade reforms
00:07:45 - Oxford Economics' Louise Loo on tariff talks 
00:14:17 - Trump tax bill hits Senate with GOP torn by competing demands
00:19:09 - US Energy Secretary Chris Wright on a peace deal with Iran   
00:24:36 - Australia cuts commodity export outlook despite gold price gain
00:28:23 - Key risks for stocks in 2H 2025
00:34:50 - Medipal Senior Managing Director Toshihide Yoda on business outlook
00:51:45 - Breaking: Japan's factory output misses expectations as US tariffs hit
00:54:23 - Masayoshi Son hints at succession plan while chasing AI ambition
00:57:22 - Markets open in Australia, South Korea & Japan
01:00:38 - UBS Global Wealth Management's Hartmut Issel on market strategy 
01:08:09 - Trump says he doesn't expect to extend tariff deadline 
01:22:25 - China June manufacturing PMIs expected to rise on trade truce with US
01:25:28 - Money managers cautious about the stock market in the second half
01:33:05 - Thai Prime Minister’s support slumps in poll amid protest 
01:40:04 - Exclusive: Noetix Robotics orders race ahead after Beijing marathon 
--------

items.title: English Football Association bans transgender women from women's soccer | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Since the beginning of this month, transgender women have been banned from playing women's football in England. Trans women had been allowed to participate in women's official games for years, though they were required to limit their testosterone levels. But after a Supreme court ruling on the definition of women, the English Football Association moved to ban trans women.

items.title: What options does Washington have to prevent Iran's nuclear advancement? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) says Iran could resume enriching uranium within months after the latest Israeli and US attacks on its nuclear facilities. 

Rafael Grossi's statement contradicted President Donald Trump's claims that last week's bombing raids had obliterated Iran's nuclear program. The Iranian parliament has suspended its cooperation with the International Atomic Energy Agency following the strikes. 

Rosemary Kelanic is the director of the Middle East program at Defense Priorities - a US-based think tank. Tehran says it has no plans to resume talks with the US. What options does Washington have now to prevent Iran's nuclear advancement? 

00:00 MidEast: How can military proliferation be prevented?  
00:44 Rosemary Kelanic, Defense Priorities think tank 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Israel intensifies strikes on Gaza City after ordering people to evacuate to the south | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Israel's armed forces have intensified their strikes on Gaza City with dozens of people injured, according to Palestinian media reports. Earlier, Israel issued evacuation orders to people in the area, telling them to head south to Al-Mawasi in Rafah. The Israeli military says it's aiming to destroy the capabilities of terrorist organizations. The new evacuation order follows intense airstrikes this weekend, with Gaza's civil defense agency reporting that Israeli strikes and gunfire killed at least 23 people on Sunday, including several women and children.

0:00 DW speaks to two aid providers in Gaza
0:38 Amjad Shawa, Head of Palestinian NGO Network
6:14 Eyad Amawi, Gaza Relief Committee

#gaza #israel #middleeast

items.title: Diplomatic efforts are underway trying to convince Syria to join the Abraham Accords | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Israel has conducted military operations across the border with its neighbor Syria, following the departure of long-time ruler Bashar al-Assad late last year. 
Israel's army has released a video of, what it says, are its forces conducting a recent ground operation in Syria. The IDF says it was targeting terrorist infrastructure linked to hostile activities near its border, raiding a weapons storage site, and arresting several suspects.
At the same time, there are diplomatic efforts -- including by US President Donald Trump -- to push Syria to join the US-brokered Abraham Accords which have normalized relations between several Arab states and Israel.

0:00 Syria didn’t publicly comment on Israel’s srikes on Iran
3:28 Nimrod Goren, President of Mitvim, The Israeli Institute for Regional Foreign Policies

#israel #syria #abrahamaccords

items.title: US, Qatar push for ceasefire even as Israel intensifies strikes on Gaza | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The US and Qatar say there is momentum towards a ceasefire deal, even as Israel has just announced it will intensify military action in Gaza. Can both of these things be true at the same time?
Meanwhile, Israels strikes on Iran have pushed international condemnation of the war in Gaza to the background. Could that have been Prime Minister Benjamin Yetanyahu's aim?

DW speaks with Mairav Zonszein, Senior Analyst on Israel-Palestine for the International Crisis Group.

items.title: Can Socialism Work for Business in NYC?
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>After this week’s democratic primary election for New York City mayor, Wall Street has more questions than answers. Partnership for New York’s President and CEO Kathryn Wylde says the disconnect between Wall Street and Zohran Mamdani’s supporters explains the attractiveness of progressive policies in a city that symbolizes capitalism. Harvard Economist Ed Glaeser has studied the success and failure of democratic socialist ideals in cities across the United States and warns that New York is facing a "maximally risky moment."
--------
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

items.title: The Price of Trump's Gold Card and Where Else You Can Live
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>The US has had a version of an investment visa since the early 20th century, so what’s new about President Trump’s "gold card" and how does it compare with other countries’ investment immigration programs? LSE’s Kristin Surak explains different incentives of countries' golden visa and passport programs and why some countries have shut them down. New Zealand’s Former Minister of Economic Development Stuart Nash explains country’s revamped investment immigration program. Cato Institute’s Alex Nowrasteh takes a different approach to immigration and says that the market is best placed to determine the right price of immigration.
--------

items.title: Why extortion and 'protection' racketeering are surging in South Africa | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>According to the Global Organized Crime Index, South Africa is sixth-worst in the world when it comes to extortion and protection racketeering. South African businesses are battling an alarming increase and the construction industry is hard hit by organized gangs demanding large sums of money - sometimes as much as 30 percent of project costs, which they claim is 'for protection.' But companies are being 'protected' by the very same gangs who are extorting them. DW’s Dianne Hawker has been following the story.

00:00 Extortion crimes soar in South Africa
04:03 DW speaks with Janine Erasmus, Website editor at Corruption Watch

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Israel orders evacuation of northern Gaza as deaths continue to mount | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Israel's military has ordered Palestinians to evacuate from parts of northern Gaza, warning of intense and imminent action. 
Posting in Arabic on X, a spokesperson for the IDF said the military would operate with intense force in Jabaliya and Gaza City, and said people should evacuate south, to Al-Mawasi, for their own safety. The IDF says it is aiming to destroy terrorist organizations.
The new evacuation order follows intense airstrikes this weekend. Citing local medical workers and the civil defence force, the DPA news agency reported that Israeli strikes have killed at least 56 people across Gaza since early Saturday. There are reports that children are amongst the dead.

00:00 Israel orders evacuation of northern Gaza
02:58 DW speaks with Shaina Low, spokesperson for the Norwegian Refugee Council

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Police clash with anti-government protesters demanding new elections | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Protesters have marched in the Serbian capital Belgrade against the government of President Aleksandar Vucic. Saturday's protests were the latest in months of anti-government demonstrations which have mostly been led by students. They began when part of a roof collapsed at a newly renovated railway station last year, resulting in 16 deaths and accusations of corruption. The protests ended with police firing tear gas after flares were thrown. DW's Idro Seferi reports from Belgrade.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Kenya protests turn deadly again one year after crackdown | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Why is history repeating itself in Kenya? One year ago, thousands took to the streets of Nairobi protesting agains new tax laws, government corruption and police brutality. Sixty people died. A year later, nothing much has changed. 
The streets of the Kenyan capital again filled with demonstrators, teargas - and gunshots. Amnesty International says sixteen more people died on Wednesday, mostly killed by the police. But each civilian life lost, is strengthening the call among Kenyans for justice, as DW correspondent Sella Oneko reports from Nairobi.

00:00 New protests in Kenya turn deadly
05:26 DW's Edith Kimani reports from Nairobi

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Mo Ibrahim on Financing in Africa, Telecoms, Sudan War
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Businessman and philanthropist Mo Ibrahim gives his take on rethinking the financing framework across the continent, outlining why he thinks good governance is crucial for attracting investments. He also take about the state of telecoms in Africa after making his multi-billion dollar exit from the sector nearly two decades ago. Ibrahim also discusses the urgent need for a solution to the conflict in his country of origin, Sudan. He speaks to Bloomberg's Jennifer Zabasajja.
--------
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

items.title: Elderly German drivers take a class in road safety for seniors | Focus on Europe
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>There’s no mandatory health check for older drivers in Germany. But when they’re involved in an accident, they’re the main cause in two out of three cases.

items.title: Senate Republicans advance Trump's 'Big Beautiful Bill' but final vote hangs in balance
items.content: <article>Senate leadership twisted arms for an initial vote on the spending plan, which still needs final approval.</article>
